---
title: Policy Gradients
description: The policy-gradient derivation via the log-derivative trick, and why
  subtracting a baseline keeps the estimator unbiased.
categories:
- Policy Optimization
---

Start from the objective

$$
\begin{align*}
J(\theta) &= \mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}[r(\tau)] \\
&= \int p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\; d\tau
\end{align*}
$$

where

$$
r(\tau) = \sum_{t=1}^{T} \gamma^{t-1} r(s_{t}, a_{t})
$$

Differentiating gives

$$
\begin{align*}
\nabla_{\theta} J(\theta) &= \nabla_{\theta} \int p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \int \nabla_{\theta} p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \int p_{\theta}(\tau)\, \nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}\!\big[\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\big] \\
&= \mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}\!\Big[\textstyle\sum_{t=1}^{T} \nabla_{\theta} \log \pi_{\theta}(a_{t} \mid s_{t})\, r(\tau)\Big]
\end{align*}
$$

using the log-derivative trick and the fact that environment dynamics do not depend on $\theta$:

$$
\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau) = \frac{\nabla_{\theta} p_{\theta}(\tau)}{p_{\theta}(\tau)}, \qquad
\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau) = \sum_{t=1}^{T} \nabla_{\theta} \log \pi_{\theta}(a_{t} \mid s_{t})
$$

A baseline can be subtracted without biasing the gradient, because the score function has zero expectation:

$$
\begin{align*}
\mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}[\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)]
&= \int p_{\theta}(\tau)\, \nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \int \nabla_{\theta} p_{\theta}(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \nabla_{\theta} \int p_{\theta}(\tau)\; d\tau \\
&= \nabla_{\theta} 1 = 0
\end{align*}
$$

so subtracting a baseline $b$ leaves the estimator unchanged in expectation:

$$
\begin{align*}
\nabla_{\theta} J(\theta)
&= \mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}\!\big[\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)\, r(\tau)\big] \\
&= \mathbb{E}_{\tau \sim p_{\theta}(\tau)}\!\big[\nabla_{\theta} \log p_{\theta}(\tau)\,(r(\tau) - b)\big]
\end{align*}
$$
